scenario_name: SOC analyst copilot with SIEM read and limited response-action tools
description: A security operations center deploys an analyst copilot. The copilot reads alerts, queries the SIEM and EDR, summarizes incidents, and (with explicit analyst confirmation) can perform low-blast-radius response actions like isolating an endpoint, blocking an indicator, or running an EDR threat-hunt query. The copilot's risk profile is asymmetric: its prompts and retrieved logs are crown-jewel sensitivity, and an attacker who already breached the SOC can use injection in logs to mislead analysts.
assumptions: Tool actions require analyst confirmation; no autonomous response.
SIEM contains content that may include PII and credentials inadvertently logged elsewhere.
Self-hosted LLM inside the SOC's privileged enclave.

components.description: Self-hosted LLM inside SOC enclave; no external egress.
Response-action tool: endpoint isolate, IOC block, hunt query. Requires analyst confirmation.
Injection-classifier on retrieved log content; secret-scanner on outputs.
SOC IDP with elevated analyst-role tokens.

risks_identified.rationale: SIEM and EDR data contain crown-jewel detection logic, secrets, and PII from incident scope.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Self-hosted; secret-scanner on outputs; residual exposure from prompt content.

risks_identified.rationale: Response-action tool can isolate hosts or block IOCs at scale; misuse has very high blast radius.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Analyst confirmation gate; per-action audit log; scope limited to non-destructive.

risks_identified.rationale: Adversary already in the environment can inject misleading content into logs to coerce analyst-confirmed response actions.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Injection-classifier on log chunks; corroboration across sources required for high-blast actions.

risks_identified.rationale: Copilot system prompt encodes the SOC's detection logic and playbook IP.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Self-hosted; access auditing; system-prompt extraction monitored.

risks_identified.rationale: Trusted-output-components manipulation: copilot might recommend masking an indicator the attacker planted.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Recommendation must include cited evidence chain; analyst reviews before action.

risks_identified.rationale: SOC operates 24/7; copilot outage affects analyst throughput during incidents.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Manual playbook continues; copilot is augmentation.

risks_identified.rationale: Audit logs of copilot reasoning are themselves crown-jewel; SOC counterintelligence concern.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Logs inside the SOC enclave; access role-gated to SOC management.

existing_controls.description: Analyst confirmation gate for every response-action tool call.
Self-hosted LLM inside SOC enclave; no external egress.
Corroboration across multiple sources required for high-blast actions.
Injection-classifier on retrieved log content; secret-scanner on outputs.
Per-action audit log of analyst identity, rationale, and tool inputs.
System-prompt extraction monitoring on copilot interactions.

control_gaps.description: No tabletop exercise of adversary-injected-log-content-driven false response.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Quarterly red-team exercise simulating injection in logs; track copilot susceptibility.

control_gaps.description: Copilot prompt-IP protection is policy-based, not technical.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Move sensitive playbook details out of the system prompt and into a retrieval-time policy module the LLM does not see verbatim.

control_gaps.description: High-blast destructive actions (mass-isolation, broad block) not explicitly excluded.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Enumerate destructive actions and require break-glass approval flow; default-deny in copilot tool set.
